subject: Ecology
title: Kelp Forests
description: A quick, practical guide to Kelp Forests.

hook.text: Kelp can grow half a meter per day, forming underwater forests taller than buildings.

buildup.text: These forests shelter hundreds of species—fish, invertebrates, otters, and seals.

discovery.text: Kelp absorbs CO₂ and nutrients, creating oases of productivity along cool coastlines.

twist.text: Sea urchins eat kelp. Without otters to eat urchins, urchin barrens replace entire forests.

climax.text: The otter-urchin-kelp chain is one of ecology's clearest examples of a trophic cascade.

punchline.text: Otters don't just float cutely. They save forests.

quiz.question: What happens to kelp forests without sea otters?
